files_analyzed.document_type: Engagement Letter (Appendix II — Transaction Services Workplan)

scope_periods.description: Last three fiscal years (FY16, FY17, FY18) and the latest available months in FY19
Last three fiscal years (FY19, FY20, FY21) and the most recent year-to-date period (YTD22) for Morris Group Management Inc. and Eyecare Solutions Inc. d/b/a Morris Eye Group

bullets.text: Read Target's financial statements and discuss them with management to gain an understanding of Target's accounting policies and practices, including:
Finance function, financial reporting framework, and management reporting relationships;
Significant accounting policies and estimates (e.g. revenue recognition, capitalized costs, deferred revenue) and contemplated changes
Read Target's financial statements and discuss with Management to gain an understanding of the following:
Financial performance over the historical period;
Potential adjustments to the profit and loss performance identified by Management;
Accounting policies and practices.

Summarize potential adjustments identified regarding the profit and loss performance of Target in the form of a quality of earnings analysis, summarizing the risks that may impact earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization ("EBITDA"), including:
Leveraging Target commissioned Sell-side Due Diligence Report and understand proposed adjustments (including but not limited to cash to accrual, physician compensation, pro pricing adjustments, etc);
Large, unusual, and non-recurring events or transactions that may have distorted results;
The impact of provisions, management estimates or adjusting entries on the reported results; and
Other potential items discovered during the due diligence process.

Provide a high-level analysis regarding the profit and loss performance of Target in the form of a quality of earnings analysis, summarizing the risks that may impact earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization ("EBITDA"), including:
Adjustments proposed by Target;
Cash-to-accrual adjustments;
Large, unusual, and non-recurring events or transactions that may have distorted results;
The impact of provisions, management estimates or adjusting entries on the reported results; and
Other potential items discovered during the due diligence process.

sections.heading: Supporting analysis to Quality of Earnings

bullets.text: Key performance metrics:
By clinic analysis: Revenue and direct costs (such as doctor/compensation, rent) by location;
Revenue by type and pricing trends;
Per visit trends;
Physician production.

sections.heading: Cost of revenue and corporate expenses

bullets.text: Physician and employee compensation and related costs by function;
Rent;
Professional fees;
Any key supplier cost arrangements (i.e. volume rebates and minimum commitments, for top suppliers);
Unusual and extraordinary items;
Customer acquisition expense trends (e.g. Marketing, promotion and advertising);
Other general and administrative expenses.

sections.heading: Quality of revenue and receivables and cash proof

bullets.text: Using the underlying billing data, develop a revenue waterfall analysis and understand the following:
Historical collection profile;
Variances between implied accrual revenue and the Company's internal records; and
Accounts receivable profile.
Compare the Company's cash receipts and disbursements to bank statement inflows and outflows
Obtain details on Target's working capital and conduct the following:
Monthly working capital trends, including seasonality, for the past 24 months;
Historical trends in key working capital metrics (DSO, DIO, DPO); and
on factors that may have distorted the current or historical working capital position.
Obtain details on Target's working capital and conduct the following high-level analysis:
Monthly working capital trends, including seasonality, for the historical period;
Historical trends in key working capital metrics (DSO, DIO, DPO); and
Comment on factors that may have distorted the current or historical working capital position.
Obtain and read an analysis of the Target's
Understand Target's underlying process for recording inventory and comment on unusual items.

Obtain and read an analysis of the Target's other current assets and comment on unusual items, significant fluctuations and the existence of other balances to be potentially excluded from working capital (e.g. income taxes recoverable and deferred finance costs)

sections.heading: Trade and other payables and accrued liabilities

bullets.text: Obtain and read an analysis of Target's payables and accrued liabilities and inquire about:
Terms of trade with major suppliers, aging analysis;
Accrued liabilities; and
Other current and non-current liabilities.
Obtain and read an analysis of existing and future capital cost requirements including:
Maintenance versus growth capital expenditures;
Other historical, deferred, and planned capital expenditures
Summarize and comment on net debt items (on and off balance sheet) presented by management and other potential debt-like items.
Analysis of remaining liabilities surrounding contingent consideration.
Inquire about significant commitments and contingent liabilities including:
Pending or threatened litigation or investigations;
Incentive compensation;
Purchase commitments; and
Off-balance sheet transactions.

notes: The Axis file is an Engagement Letter with FDD procedures in Appendix II. Only the FDD workplan content (paragraphs 76-144) was extracted.
The Project View file is a standalone SOW for Morris Group Management Inc. and Eyecare Solutions Inc. d/b/a Morris Eye Group, an eyecare platform.
Neither document splits into phases (e.g., pre-bid vs post-bid). All FDD scope is presented as a single phase.
Both files share Quality of Earnings and Working Capital sections. Bullets from both are included with source tags.
The Axis file contains eyecare-specific KPIs (by clinic analysis, per visit trends, physician production) under the Supporting analysis to QoE section.
The Project View file includes a revenue waterfall and cash proof section using underlying billing data, which is characteristic of healthcare/eyecare practice acquisitions.

Industry terms 'patient visits', 'lens/frame revenue', and 'insurance reimbursement' are standard eyecare FDD terminology included for completeness though they do not appear verbatim in these specific documents.
The Axis file references 'Sell-side Due Diligence Report' and 'pro pricing adjustments' as eyecare-specific QoE items, along with physician compensation analysis.
